Package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.view

Interface View

All Known Implementing Classes:
FreemarkerView, JspView, MustacheView, ThymeleafView
public interface View
Engine-agnostic contract for a server-side-rendered template return value from an @RestOp-annotated method.

A View carries the information a templating engine needs to render a response: the template name (relative to a base path supplied by the corresponding mixin), an attribute map exposed to the template, and an optional response-headers seam for setting Content-Type, Cache-Control, or other headers on the rendered response.

The interface is deliberately minimal and engine-agnostic. Concrete implementations live in the per-engine bridge modules (juneau-rest-server-view-jsp, future juneau-rest-server-view-thymeleaf / -mustache / -freemarker), each paired with a ResponseProcessor that detects its own View subtype and dispatches to the underlying templating engine.

End-state usage:

@Rest(path="/app", mixins=JspMixin.class) public class AppResource extends RestServlet { @RestGet("/hello/{name}") public View hello(@Path String name) { return JspView.of("hello.jsp") .attr("name", name) .attr("ts", Instant.now()); } }

Contract:
  • getTemplateName() — the template path the engine should render, typically relative to a mixin-configured base path (e.g. "hello.jsp" with base path "/WEB-INF/views/" resolves to "/WEB-INF/views/hello.jsp"). Must never be null.
  • getAttributes() — key/value bindings exposed to the template (e.g. ${name} in a JSP, th:text="${name}" in a Thymeleaf template). Returns an immutable empty map when no attributes are bound. Must never be null.
  • getResponseHeaders() — optional response-headers map applied by the renderer before forwarding to the engine. Defaults to an empty map so most implementations don't need to override it; engines that want to set Content-Type or Cache-Control on the rendered response can populate this.
Stability:

This interface is the stable extension point for view modules. New methods will be added as default-bodied where possible to preserve backward compatibility with downstream view impls.

See Also:
  • JSP View Support
  • REST Server — Composition (mixins, paths)
Since:
10.0.0

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    Map<String,Object>
    getAttributes()
    The attributes to expose to the template.
    default Map<String,String>
    getResponseHeaders()
    Optional response headers applied by the renderer before forwarding to the templating engine.
    String
    getTemplateName()
    The name of the template to render — typically a path relative to the mixin-configured base path.

  • Method Details

    • getTemplateName

      String getTemplateName()
      The name of the template to render — typically a path relative to the mixin-configured base path.

      For example, "hello.jsp" with a JSP mixin basePath("/WEB-INF/views/") resolves to "/WEB-INF/views/hello.jsp" on the request dispatcher.

      Returns:
      The template name. Never null.

    • getAttributes

      Map<String,Object> getAttributes()
      The attributes to expose to the template.

      Each entry is forwarded to the templating engine as a named binding accessible from inside the template (e.g. ${name} in JSP / EL, th:text="${name}" in Thymeleaf).

      Returns:
      The attributes map. Never null; returns an immutable empty map when no attributes are bound.

    • getResponseHeaders

      default Map<String,String> getResponseHeaders()
      Optional response headers applied by the renderer before forwarding to the templating engine.

      Typical use cases: setting Content-Type (e.g. "text/html; charset=UTF-8") or Cache-Control on the rendered response. The default implementation returns an empty map so most View impls don't need to override it.

      Returns:
      An immutable map of headers to apply. Never null; defaults to an empty map.
